Name of the department

Foreign key linking to the role

Notes or details about the maintenance performed

entity_relationships.business_meaning: An employee belongs to one department
An equipment item is classified under one equipment type
A turnaround operation is performed for one flight
A baggage item is associated with one flight

business_processes.tables: attendance, departmentsss, employee, employee_role, leave_request, role, schedule, shift, training_record

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround Management
use_cases.description: Coordinates all ground operations for a flight's arrival and departure including gate/stand assignment, refueling, catering, cleaning, boarding preparation, and checklist completion to ensure on-time departure.

use_cases.name: Employee Attendance and Shift Management
use_cases.description: Tracks employee check-in/check-out times against scheduled shifts, manages leave requests, and monitors attendance compliance across departments.

use_cases.name: Baggage Tracking and Tracing
use_cases.description: Provides end-to-end visibility of baggage from check-in through loading, in-transit scans, unloading, and handles lost baggage incidents with status tracking.

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance Management
use_cases.description: Manages equipment lifecycle including type categorization, assignment to employees, maintenance request workflow, and historical maintenance records for operational readiness.

use_cases.name: Crew Scheduling and Training Compliance
use_cases.description: Assigns qualified crew to shifts based on roles, tracks training completion and certifications, and ensures regulatory compliance for operational staff.

validation_report.message: Foreign key employee.department_id references table 'department', which does not exist in the schema.
